Feature: Host Session Decoupling and Integration

  This feature verifies that Corvus Star decouples its intelligence layer from
  direct SDK assumptions and routes through the shared Mimir/Uplink contract
  across supported host providers.

  Scenario: AntigravityUplink returns structured intelligence through the shared Mimir bridge
    Given the AntigravityUplink is invoked with a query and persona context
    When the shared Mimir client returns a successful intelligence response
    Then the uplink should return a "success" status
    And it should surface the raw intelligence payload under "data.raw"
    And it should preserve the bridge trace metadata including "transport_mode"

  Scenario: Bootstrap preserves active host-session markers across the control plane
    Given the environment declares an active host provider and session markers
    When the shared bootstrap awakens the estate
    Then the bootstrap should preserve those host markers in process environment
    And downstream spokes should not need to re-infer the host from Gemini-only state

  Scenario: Control Plane (cstar.ts) displays provider-specific integration status
    Given the environment declares an active host provider
    When the "status" command is executed
    Then the output should include the matching host integration banner
    And the control plane should not assume Gemini is the only interactive host

  Scenario: Provider-specific host bridges honor configured commands and preserve transport metadata
    Given the environment declares an explicit host bridge command for a supported provider
    When the shared Mimir client fulfills a request through that bridge
    Then the request should execute through the configured provider bridge
    And the response trace should preserve "transport_mode" as "host_session"
